companies.shortDescription:
Festo Sdn. Berhad operates as an automation machinery manufacturing company. The Company offers motors and servo drives, actuators, industrial robots, sensors, compressed air preparation, valve terminals, pneumatic connection technology, regulators, electronic controllers, and other related products. Festo serves customers in Malaysia. || Group of companies in the area of control and automation technology with headquarter in Esslingen am Neckar | Festo was founded in 1925 by Albert Fezer and Gottlieb Stoll in Esslingen am Neckar, Germany., initially specializing in wood-processing machinery. The company was named after its founders Albert Fezer and Gottlieb Stoll. | Festo is a global player and an independent family-owned company with headquarters in Esslingen am Neckar, Germany. Festo has set standards in industrial automation technology and technical education ever since its establishment, thereby making a contribution to sustainable development of the environment, the economy and society. The company supplies pneumatic and electrical automation technology to 300,000 customers of factory and process automation in over 35 industries. The LifeTech sector with medical technology and laboratory automation is becoming increasingly important. The products and services are available in 176 countries. With about 20,700 employees in over 250 branch offices in 61 countries worldwide, Festo achieved a turnover of around €3.36 billion in 2021. Each year around 7% of this turnover is invested in research and development. In this learning company, 1.5 % of turnover is invested in basic and further training. Festo Didactic SE is a leading provider of technical education and training and offers its customers worldwide comprehensive digital and physical learning solutions in the industrial environment.

companies.name: SENSUM
companies.shortDescription: Sensum develops and manufactures automatic visual inspection machines and computer vision systems for pharmaceutical and nutraceutical companies. The company focuses on 100% quality inspection and sorting of tablets, capsules, transparent capsules and softgels, as well as counting and bottle filling. Sensum also provides lifting, dedusting and polishing equipment for solid dosage forms and process analytical technology systems for automated particle size and shape analysis in fluid bed and related processes. Its machines use computer vision, image processing and, more recently, deep learning to classify products and separate defective from acceptable units. Ihr Partner für die Projektanalyse und -umsetzung
Jedes Projektengagement von ADS Engineering wird durch die Bedürfnisse der Kunden definiert. Sie wählen aus unserem reichhaltigen Fundus von Fachwissen und Erfahrung gezielt die benötigten Kompetenzen. Dabei le-gen wir höchsten Wert auf Qualität und Effizienz.

companies.name: Sweeper
companies.shortDescription: SWEEPER’s main objective is to put the first generation greenhouse harvesting robots onto the market. Until now this has never been achieved and it will ensure Europe’s leading role in agricultural robotics.  In modern greenhouses there is a high demand to automate labour. The availability of a skilled workforce that accepts repetitive tasks in the harsh climate conditions of a greenhouse is decreasing rapidly. The current state of the art in automated harvesting of fruits and vegetables has remained remarkably stationary in the past decades. In the EU-FP7-project CROPS (www.crops-robots.eu) extensive research has been performed on agricultural robotics. One of the applications was a sweet pepper harvesting robot. SWEEPER will use the technology developed in CROPS to introduce, test and validate a robotic harvesting solution for sweet pepper under real-world conditions.  The project involves 6 partners from 4 different countries (The Netherlands, Belgium, Sweden and Israel). The consortium consist out of fundamental and applied research organisations, a system integrator and a modern grower of sweet pepper. In the consortium a wide-range of disciplines are available, including: horticulture, horticultural engineering, machine vision, sensing, robotics, control, intelligent systems, software architecture, system integration and greenhouse crop management. || SWEEPER’s main objective is to put the first generation greenhouse harvesting robots onto the market. | SWEEPER’s main objective is to put the first generation greenhouse harvesting robots onto the market. We are the specialists in visual inspection and identification solutions. As a reliable and enthusiastic partner, we provide turn-key solutions tailored to your specific requirements and wishes. As an integrator, we strive to implement the best-suited solution within your existing production and packaging processes. We collaborate with you as a manufacturer or OEM, aiming for a long-term partnership for the future.

We view quality assurance as “continuous improvement,” keeping it in focus and helping to further develop it within organizations.

Our ViTrack software provides full insight into inspection data and tracks all completed inspection points. The entire system can be managed from your own workstation, with data and images stored as verifiable evidence. Thanks to our API, integration with any ERP system is seamless and straightforward. Our proprietary software is fully developed in-house and is 100% suitable for industrial production processes.

Die ADOMO Technologie wird seit Jahren im Automotive Bereich eingesetzt, findet aber mittlerweile in allen Industrien in den Bereichen Traceability und Track&Trace seinen Einsatz. We're hiring! https://fizyr.com/careers/ | Computer vision software- with native artificial intelligence and machine learning - built for picking & stacking items, parcels, depalletizing and trailer unloading. Fizyr designs, builds, installs, and maintains an unmatched vision software product for automated picking & stacking items, parcels, depalletizing, and trailer unloading. Our deep learning algorithms are used to enable automated handling of unknown objects varying in shape, size, color, material, and stacking, being picked from (conveyed) bulk. Fizyr’s product is applied in production all over the world by leading system integrators and end-users, in online retail, warehousing and parcel services.  

We started in 2014 as ‘Delft Robotics’, a TU Delft spin-off founded by Professor Martijn Wisse, as a system integrator building robotic picking cells. In 2016, we participated in and won both competitions of the Amazon Picking challenge, mainly due to our track record in applying deep learning. This led to the company pivoting towards a deep learning computer vision software-only product and re-branding to Fizyr in 2017.  

Our Plug-and-Play modular product allows for a smooth integration with any system. We give integrators the freedom to choose the best hardware for their picking cell and we help them configure it, while facilitating cost, robustness, and speed optimization. Our algorithms provide over 100 grasp poses each second, including classification to handle objects differently, but also perform quality controls and detect defects to prevent damaged items from being placed on a sorter.  

Leading integrators across the globe acknowledged our product’s high quality and used it in production. This is what makes us truly unique; we were trusted as a strategic partner and grew successfully bootstrapped since the beginning. Up to this day, we remain dedicated to creating true value for our partners, so that they can create cutting-edge logistics solutions for their clients.

Get the most technically advanced robot in the world for quality control, breeding and fine sorting. The QSorter® is packed with features that deliver impressive accuracy and consistency in the measurement of quality parameters of seeds, grains and beans. Our 3D Vision camera and the high resolution spectroscopy sensor can detect the most challenging physical details and several biochemical properties. The quality parameters predictions are based AI algorithms, whose accuracy over time.

Simply log in the SQI2 Cloud platform to access all the data and share them with collaborators, partners and customers in no time. 

Say goodbye to subjectivity and sampling errors, to lengthy and expensive quality inspection protocols, to disagreements between stakeholders and start using QSorter®. Enable the selection of crops with respect to biochemical properties or upgrade lots by removing defected or unwanted beans. 

Start using the QSorter® today

companies.name: xis.ai
companies.shortDescription:
xis.ai specializes in automating visual quality inspection and process monitoring for industrial applications using artificial intelligence and robotics. Our primary focus is on revolutionizing industrial quality inspection through advanced computer vision technology.

The company offers a comprehensive suite of software and hardware solutions designed to streamline the development and deployment of AI-powered visual inspection systems:

1. Software:
   - xis dev: A no-code platform that allows non-technical users to develop computer vision models without coding knowledge. It includes features like AI-assisted labelling, model training, and performance analytics.
   - xis Runtime: A lightweight platform for deploying computer vision models in production environments, offering real-time analytics and integration with various industrial cameras and sensors.

2. Hardware:
   - Edge AI: Deployment of AI algorithms directly on edge devices like cameras or sensors for real-time analysis and faster decision-making.
   - Support for various visual sensors: Industrial cameras, microscopes, thermal cameras, and X-ray tubes.

xis.ai's platform is designed to be user-friendly, enabling non-technical industrial professionals to develop and implement AI-based visual inspection solutions quickly and efficiently. This approach aims to make advanced quality control technologies more accessible to various industries and applications. || xis.ai specializes in automating visual quality inspection and process monitoring for industrial applications using artificial intelligence and robotics. The company focuses on metrology grade inspection delivered through 3D laser triangulation sensors. By providing global manufacturers with solutions that improve their product quality, SmartRay optimizes their processes, and reduce their production costs. SmartRay is split into two successful business units: Sensor technology: ECCO sensors, and JOSY the complete solution for weld seam inspection.

The leading-edge ECCO sensors provide highly precise 3D laser measurement, repeatable inspection data, creating detailed 3D images. With  precise sensors, SmartRay helps well-known companies worldwide with process optimization and automated measurement tasks.

These first-class sensors and the associated software forms the core of JOSY – an all-in-one turnkey solution for non-contact visual 3D inspections. SmartRay is the global market leader in high end weld seam inspection. Large manufacturers, especially the leading automotive companies, rely on SmartRay’s expertise.

For more details, visit SmartRay at www.smartray.com | SmartRay specialise in 3D sensors* for inspection, guidance and measurement; that help manufacturing companies improve product quality, guide automation and reduce production costs. By focusing on 3D, SmartRay has built a comprehensive portfolio of products designed to solve a rapidly increasing range of industrial applications. The company’s strategy is to combine new technologies with German engineering to create a range of 3D sensors that can be fitted anywhere, are quick to set up and easy to deploy. 

SmartRay sensors combine laser triangulation with innovative image formation technology to create detailed 3D images that can be processed by any 3rd party vision software. The latest ECCOTM family brings a new design philosophy to the 3D sensor market that delivers high performance, small size, lightweight and stylish packaging. SmartRay 3D technology also forms the core of the WeldVisionTM high speed inline weld inspection systems, employed by major automotive manufacturers to verify weld seam quality.

Based in Munich area, Germany, the company’s global coverage is expanding rapidly through a network of distributors and system integrators.  For more details, visit SmartRay at www.smartray.de

About Us
AWETA was conceived out of one man’s desire to help vegetable growers in the Netherlands accurately grade their cucumbers and tomatoes. He developed a mechanical sorting machine, started in-house production of it and gave his name to the company AWETA (Adrianus Wilhelmus Tas).
	
o	The first sorting machines on the market for cucumbers, tomatoes and cherry tomatoes, peppers, apples, pears, roses, fish and citrus fruit
o	Combined colour and diameter sorting machine for tomatoes
o	First combined sealing and shrink wrapping machine for cucumbers
o	First color/diameter camera for apples (CCD)
o	The Falcon system; the first quality sorting machine for apples
